data.narrative:
Blue Origin, a private space and aerospace company founded by Jeff Bezos, commands a stated valuation of $30B+ despite having raised $10B+ to date. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 13 companies spanning public and private tiers, including five publicly traded space operators (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers such as SpaceX, Relativity Space, and Firefly Aerospace.

Only one triangulation method proved applicable to this analysis. The peer-median approach across three same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables—Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), and Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B)—yielded a median implied valuation of $7.0B. The public-comp anchoring method, which references a sector P/S median of 53.2x across six public tickers, could not be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue figures for the private target. Revenue disclosure remains rare among private companies at this scale.

The triangulated valuation range spans $6.0B to $8.0B, centered on $7.0B, based solely on peer-median evidence. This analysis reveals a material gap between the stated $30B+ valuation and comparable-company indications. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data necessary for alternative valuation methods, the wide dispersion in the peer sample ($6.3B to $37.0B), and limited visibility into Blue Origin's financial performance and near-term commercialization timelines relative to public comparables.
